raw_excerpt_key_facts: VERIFIER RECAPTURE FINDING: The PDF returns as raw binary via webfetch (the same edge case the Researcher flagged). The first ~1KB of the response shows the PDF binary header '%PDF-1.6' followed by FlateDecode-compressed object streams. The PDF is multi-page (Pages catalog references /PageLayout /OneColumn and /MediaBox 612x792 = US Letter). The text content is encoded in PDF font objects (TT0, TT1, TT2, TT3, TT4 = 5 embedded TrueType font subsets) with /FlateDecode compression, which webfetch does not decompress. CONFIRMS the Researcher's report that 'PDFs returned via webfetch as raw binary are a known edge case.' No way to extract the 12-peptide enumeration from this PDF without an actual PDF parser (pdftotext, pdf2text, or similar).
diff_against_researcher_capture: Both the Researcher and the Verifier confirm the PDF is unreadable as text via webfetch. The Researcher's mitigation (accept secondary-corroboration with weight reduction) is the correct call here. The 12-peptide enumeration is independently corroborated by Frier Levitt and Orrick, both of which attribute the list to the FDA's republished 503A bulks list.
